property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: these are not functional interfaces they just happen to only have one abstract method :) pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.Command') or pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.CommandContextTransformer') or pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.parameter.ParameterConverter') or pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.parameter.ParameterParser') or pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.restriction.Restriction') or pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.slash.javacord.SlashCommandJavacord') or pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.slash.jda.SlashCommandJda') ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: a dummy parameter for finalizer attack prevention :) parent::FormalParameter and (@Name = "parametersValidated") and ancestor::ConstructorDeclaration ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: false positives, work-around for https://github.com/pmd/pmd/issues/6274 :) (ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.util.SlashCommandBuilderProducer')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'getSlashCommandBuilders' and ancestor::MethodCall/@MethodName = "groupingBy") or (ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.util.SlashCommandDatasProducer')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'getSlashCommandDatas' and ancestor::MethodCall/@MethodName = "groupingBy") ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: CDI observer method to ensure initialization at startup :) ((@Name = 'ensureInitializationAtStartup') and descendant::FormalParameter[ pmd-java:hasAnnotation('jakarta.enterprise.event.Observes') and pmd-java:hasAnnotation('jakarta.enterprise.context.Initialized')]) (: the subclasses should not be forced to provide implementations :) or (((@Name = 'expression') or (@Name = 'optional') or (@Name = 'placeholder') or (@Name = 'placeholderWithWhitespace') or (@Name = 'alternatives') or (@Name = 'literal')) and ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.usage.UsageParserRuleContext')]) ]

property.name: ignoredAnnotations
property.value: java.lang.Override, java.lang.Deprecated

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: this is a logical or combination, so the short name is perfectly fine :) (@Name = 'or') and ClassType[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.restriction.RestrictionChainElement')] ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: maybe could be refactored or removed at some point, but not now :) descendant::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.test.pitest.SpockTestUnitFinder')] ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: all behavior is inside the class already :) pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.Version') ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: might qualify as god class, maybe untangle at some point, but not now :) pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.CommandHandler') ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: the method is for consumers of the two subclasses :) ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.util.lazy.LazyReference')] and self::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'isSet' ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: the exception gets rethrown as runtime exception :) ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.parameter.ParameterConverter')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'convert' ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: it is better readable like it is :) ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.test.pitest.DuplicateMutationFilter')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'intercept' and descendant::LambdaParameter/VariableId/@Name = "seenBytes" ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: the exception gets rethrown as runtime exception :) ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.parameter.parser.TypedParameterParser')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'convertTokenValue' ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: these should all be listed explicitly without using constants :) ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.test.pitest.ExplicitMutationFilter')] and ancestor::VariableDeclarator/@Name = 'filter' ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: it's ok for Version to have a version field :) ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.Version')] ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: not a test class :) pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.test.pitest.SpockTestUnitFinder.TestIdentifierListener') ]

property.name: violationSuppressXPath
property.value: .[ (: these are not used concurrently :) (ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.test.pitest.DuplicateMutationFilter')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'intercept' and ancestor::LocalVariableDeclaration/VariableDeclarator/@Name = 'seen') or (ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.api.CommandContext.Builder')] and ancestor::FieldDeclaration/VariableDeclarator/@Name = 'additionalData') or (ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.parameter.parser.TypedParameterParser')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'parseParameters' and ancestor::LocalVariableDeclaration/VariableDeclarator/@Name = 'result') or (ancestor::ClassDeclaration[pmd-java:typeIsExactly('net.kautler.command.parameter.parser.UntypedParameterParser')] and ancestor::MethodDeclaration/@Name = 'parse' and ancestor::LocalVariableDeclaration/VariableDeclarator/@Name = 'parameters') ]

rule.name: UseLogBuilderStyle
rule.description: Use log builder style consistently. For example - instead of "catching(...)", use "at<Level>().withThrowable(...).log()" - instead of "<level>(...)" or "log(<level>, ...)", use "at<Level>().log(...)" - instead of "atLevel(<level>)", use "at<Level>()" - do not use "is<level>Enabled()" or "isEnabled(<level>) at all, instead use "Supplier" variants of the "log" method to only do costly message calculation if the message is actually going to be logged

property.name: xpath

rule.name: UseStringLiteralAsFirstArgumentToLogMethod
rule.message: Use a String literal as first argument to log() method
rule.description: Use a String literal as first argument to log() method, not for example a concatenation or a method call (except if it is a compile-time constant), use placeholders and arguments instead.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: UseAsManyArgumentsAsPlaceholdersInLogMethod
rule.message: Use as many arguments as placeholders in log() method
rule.description: Use as many arguments as placeholders in log() method, a mismatch in amount is usually a bug.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: UseOnlySimpleAccessesOrSuppliersAsArgumentsForLogMethod
rule.message: Use only simple accesses or Suppliers as arguments for log() method
rule.description: Use only simple accesses or Suppliers as arguments for log() method. Ternaries or method calls are wasted time if the message is not going to be logged, String literals should be part of the message in the first argument.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //MethodCall [@MethodName = "log"] [ head(*)/descendant::VariableAccess[pmd-java:typeIs("org.apache.logging.log4j.Logger")] or head(*)/descendant-or-self::VariableAccess[pmd-java:typeIs("org.apache.logging.log4j.LogBuilder")] ] [tail(ArgumentList/*)/self::*[not(self::VariableAccess or self::LambdaExpression or self::MethodReference)]]
